reasoning: South Korea's total fertility rate in 2023 was 0.72. According to Statistics Korea, the rate rebounded in 2024 to 0.75, backed by a significant post-pandemic increase in marriages. The trend has continued strongly into 2025. By July 2025, births had grown year-over-year for 13 consecutive months. In the first half of 2025, births grew by 7.4% compared to the same period in 2024, setting a record growth rate, with Q1 2025 TFR reaching 0.82. Since the first seven months of 2025 have strongly outperformed 2024 (which already beat the 0.72 threshold), it is almost mathematically impossible for the final 2025 TFR to fall down to or below 0.72 without an unprecedented collapse in births in the last 4-5 months of the year. Given demographic inertia and the continued reporting of increases up to Q3 2025, a Yes resolution is virtually assured.
